summary: PR 2738 improved GALE01 data matching across config symbols/splits and several source files, primarily by refining data symbol boundaries/scopes, adding or reshaping data definitions, adding padding wrapper structs, and using narrow ordering helpers for data/sdata/sdata2 placement. The bot report recorded +0.13% matched data and 12 new matched data items.

key_files.role: Moved gmregtyfall .sdata2 split boundary so 0x804DAA18-0x804DAA28 belongs to gm_1A4C rather than gmregtyfall.
Adjusted symbol sizes/scopes and split large data regions into smaller local symbols for lbdvd, gmregtyfall, leak, and grgreatbay-related data.

Major data-layout work: reordered includes, surfaced numerous globals with address comments, split Vec3 data, added typed padding wrappers for image descriptor arrays, and added data/sdata2 ordering helper functions.
Changed data layout for baselib leak unit; full hunk not loaded, but bot report shows .data moved from 0% to 100%.
Wrapped __jmp_buf in a padded struct, changed setjmp/longjmp accesses to .buf, and added a documented .sdata order hack.
Added ftCo_DownBound_SfxIds data, changed a dummy stack array to PAD_STACK(8), and adjusted includes.
Added PlATK enum with AttackNormal start/end values.
Include cleanup related to item headers; bot report records grgreatbay .sbss reaching 100%.
Changed one line; loaded diff is truncated before the hunk, but bot report records pltrick .data reaching 100%.

what_changed: Updated GALE01 splits so gmregtyfall .sdata2 ends at 0x804DAA18 and gm_1A4C owns an added .sdata2 range from 0x804DAA18 to 0x804DAA28.

Refined symbols.txt entries by splitting objects, changing object sizes, and changing some symbols from global to local scope, including lbDvd_803B72C0/lbl_803B72CC, gm_803DB2D4/gm_803DB2E0, HSD_Leak_80407B58 plus many local string symbols, lbDvd_804D37E4/EC/F4, gm_804D4278, and grGb_804D69E0.

In gmregtyfall.c, converted several static or extern declarations into address-commented file/global declarations near the top of the file and reshaped two image descriptor arrays into padded wrapper structs.
In gmregtyfall.c, replaced raw 2D array indexing of gm_804808F8 and gm_80480964 with accesses through the wrapper member x0.
In gmregtyfall.c and granime.c, added small static order helper functions using void casts to literals/strings/symbols to affect section ordering.
In granime.c, modeled grAnime_8049EE40 as a padded struct containing __jmp_buf and updated setjmp/longjmp call sites to use grAnime_8049EE40.buf.
In ftCo_DownBound.c, added an enum_t array ftCo_DownBound_SfxIds[] = { 9, 10, 11, 12 } and replaced a zeroed dummy stack buffer with PAD_STACK(8).
Added PlATK enum values PlATK_AttackNormal_Start = 1 and PlATK_AttackNormal_End = 16 to pl/forward.h.
Adjusted includes in gmregtyfall.c, ftCo_DownBound.c, grgreatbay.c, and lbarq.c.
smart_moves: Used config symbol boundary edits together with source declarations, rather than only source churn, to improve data matching across multiple units.
Modeled BSS padding as explicit wrapper structs in gmregtyfall and granime, making padding part of the declared object shape rather than separate anonymous nearby globals.
Replaced a dummy local stack buffer with PAD_STACK(8), which is clearer about stack-padding intent than an unused zero-initialized array.
Kept several newly exposed data names address-style, such as gm_804D67AC and lbl_80407B78, rather than inventing unsupported semantic names.

decomp_lessons.lesson: When data matching depends on object boundaries, symbols.txt size/scope changes and splits.txt ownership changes may be part of the match, not just C source edits.
For arrays followed by fixed padding, a temporary wrapper struct can express the true object extent while preserving typed access through a named member.
Prefer PAD_STACK for intentional stack padding over anonymous dummy local buffers when padding is retained for matching.

naming_conventions.convention: Address-style names were retained for newly surfaced or split data objects where the PR evidence does not show semantic meaning, such as gm_804D67AC, gm_804808F8, HSD_Leak_80407B58, and lbl_80407B78.
Padding wrapper members used mechanical field names such as x0 and x60, matching an incomplete-layout convention rather than over-naming unknown fields.
The added PlATK enum names use a subsystem prefix and range-style names for start/end constants: PlATK_AttackNormal_Start and PlATK_AttackNormal_End.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Adjusted split ownership and symbol size/scope metadata to align data sections before or alongside C source changes.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk_or_note: Useful for data matching, but object ownership should remain tied to section/symbol evidence rather than convenience.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Used static no-call ordering helpers with void-cast literals or symbols, e.g. order_sdata2, order_data, and order_sdata, to influence section ordering.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk_or_note: This is explicitly hack-like in granime and intersects with standards warning against data-order globals/literal ownership without evidence.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Modeled data/BSS padding by embedding a pad array in a wrapper struct, then accessing the real object through a member such as .x0 or .buf.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk_or_note: Appropriate as a narrow layout tactic when object size evidence supports it; should not become a general replacement for recovering real types.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Used PAD_STACK(8) for stack padding in ftCo_DownBound_Coll.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk_or_note: Retained padding tactics require match evidence; the bot report supports related data improvement but does not isolate this function's codegen effect.

follow_up_queries: Search for order_sdata/order_sdata2/order_data helper functions to see whether void-cast ordering hacks are recurring and whether they need documented guardrails.
Search for padded wrapper structs around __jmp_buf or HSD_ImageDesc arrays to identify reusable layout patterns or cleanup opportunities.
Search for dummy stack arrays replaced by PAD_STACK to determine whether similar cleanup remains elsewhere.
Inspect the full pr-2738 diff for src/sysdolphin/baselib/leak.c and src/melee/pl/pltrick.c, which are changed but truncated in the loaded diff excerpt.

notes: PR body is empty and there are no human review comments in the loaded slice.
The diff excerpt is truncated before the pltrick hunk and does not include the full baselib/leak.c changes, so file-specific lessons for those files are limited.
The bot report provides quantitative data-match evidence, including +1560 bytes and 12 new matches, but it does not prove author intent for individual tactics.

Some retained tactics, especially order_sdata/order_data helpers, are supported as PR evidence but should not be promoted as accepted style without curator review because loaded standards caution against data-order hacks and literal/data ownership shortcuts.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Config symbol/split metadata can be central to data-match PRs
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR 2738 improved data matches by changing both source and GALE01 config metadata: symbols.txt split/resize/scope edits and splits.txt .sdata2 ownership changes accompanied source declarations and padding layout changes.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Use PAD_STACK instead of dummy local buffers for retained stack padding
accepted_candidate_records.text: In ftCo_DownBound_Coll, PR 2738 replaced `u8 _[8] = { 0 };` with `PAD_STACK(8);`, making the retained padding tactic explicit.

source_update_candidates.title: Clarify treatment of void-cast data ordering helper functions
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2738 used static helper functions such as `order_sdata2`, `order_data`, and `order_sdata` containing `(void)` casts of floats, strings, or symbols to influence data/sdata/sdata2 ordering. One was explicitly marked `/// @todo .sdata order hack`. Consider documenting whether these are permitted temporary matching tactics, what evidence is required, and when they should be replaced by better symbol/split ownership.
source_update_candidates.reason: Loaded standards already warn about data-order globals/literal ownership and tactics needing evidence, but this PR shows a specific recurring idiom that may need explicit curator guidance.

source_update_candidates.title: Document padded wrapper structs as a temporary data/BSS layout tactic
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2738 modeled object extents with wrapper structs containing the real object plus explicit padding, e.g. `struct ImageDesc_Array { HSD_ImageDesc x0[2][2]; u8 x60[0xC]; }` and `struct padded_jmp_buf { __jmp_buf buf; u8 pad[0x118 - 0xF8]; }`. Consider documenting when wrapper structs are acceptable and how to avoid hiding recoverable real types.
source_update_candidates.reason: The pattern is evidence-backed in this PR but should be reviewed as a tactic rather than promoted automatically as normal authored style.

source_update_candidates.title: gmregtyfall data matching depends on several explicit data/BSS layout objects
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2738 surfaced gmregtyfall globals with address comments, split Vec3 data at gm_803DB2C8/gm_803DB2D4/gm_803DB2E0, reshaped gm_804808F8 and gm_80480964 as padded ImageDesc_Array objects, and adjusted .sdata2 split ownership with gm_1A4C.
source_update_candidates.reason: Potentially useful path-local context, but requires curator validation against current source before adding as a path fact.

rejection_notes: Do not promote the order_sdata/order_data helpers as accepted style from this PR alone; the loaded PR evidence shows them as matching tactics, including one explicit TODO hack, not as a standard.
Do not infer semantic meanings for address-style symbols such as lbl_80407B78, gm_804D67AC, or grGb_804D69E0 beyond their data addresses/scopes/sizes shown in the diff.
Do not claim human reviewer approval of any tactic; the loaded slice has no review comments.
